---
title: "Project 4_table"
author: "Lu Bai"
date: "2026-05-10"
output: word_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(dplyr)
library(flextable)
```

## Load results

```{r}
load("~/Downloads/project4_simresults.RData")
```

## Generate Table
```{r}
tab_df <- bind_rows(all_results, .id = "Scenario") %>%
  # Ensure metrics are numeric for calculation
  mutate(across(c(TPR:Est_X6), as.numeric)) %>%
  group_by(Scenario, Method) %>%
  summarise(
    # Calculate the mean for all performance metrics
    across(c(TPR:Est_X6), mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    # Logic for summarizing Selected Variables:
    # Identify which variables appeared most often across the 10,000 iterations
    Commonly_Selected = {
      # Split strings, unlist to individual variable IDs, and count frequencies
      all_vars <- unlist(strsplit(as.character(Selected_Vars), ","))
      all_vars <- all_vars[all_vars != "" & !is.na(all_vars)]
      
      if(length(all_vars) == 0) {
        "None"
      } else {
        var_counts <- sort(table(all_vars), decreasing = TRUE)
        # Keep variables selected in more than 50% of the iterations
        top_vars <- names(var_counts[var_counts > (iterations / 2)])
        paste(sort(as.numeric(top_vars)), collapse = ",")
      }
    },
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# Reorder columns
tab_df <- tab_df %>% 
  relocate(Scenario, Method, Commonly_Selected)

# Identify the 5 largest and 5 smallest coverage methods
top_5_cov <- tab_df %>% slice_max(order_by = Coverage, n = 5)
bot_5_cov <- tab_df %>% slice_min(order_by = Coverage, n = 5)

# Combine for the final 10-row subset
selected_methods <- bind_rows(top_5_cov, bot_5_cov) %>%
  arrange(desc(Coverage))

# Table1: performance metrics
table1_ft <- selected_methods %>%
  select(Scenario, Method, Commonly_Selected, TPR, FPR, Bias, Coverage, TypeI, TypeII) %>%
  flextable() %>%
  set_header_labels(
    Commonly_Selected = "Commonly Selected Variables (>50%)",
    Bias = "Mean Bias",
    Coverage = "95% CI Coverage",
    TypeI = "Type I Error",
    TypeII = "Type II Error"
  ) %>%
  colformat_double(digits = 3) %>%
  set_caption("Table 1: Selection Performance Metrics for Methods with Extreme Coverage") %>%
  theme_vanilla() %>%
  autofit()

table1_ft

# Table2: coefficients estimates
# Extract the estimate
table2_data <- selected_methods %>%
  mutate(
    # Extract the estimate
    across(starts_with("Est_X"), ~ sprintf("%.3f", .x))
  ) %>%
  # Select the scenario, method, and variables X1-X6
  select(Scenario, Method, Commonly_Selected, Est_X1, Est_X2, Est_X3, Est_X4, Est_X5, Est_X6)

table2_ft <- table2_data %>%
  flextable() %>%
  set_header_labels(
    Commonly_Selected = "Commonly Selected Variables (>50%)",
    Est_X1 = "X1 (β=0.167)",
    Est_X2 = "X2 (β=0.333)",
    Est_X3 = "X3 (β=0.500)",
    Est_X4 = "X4 (β=0.667)",
    Est_X5 = "X5 (β=0.833)",
    Est_X6 = "X6 (β=0.000)"
  ) %>%
  set_caption("Table 2: Mean Coefficient Estimates for Selected Methods") %>%
  theme_vanilla() %>%
  autofit()

table2_ft
```

## Identify specific algorithms given metrics
```{r}
# Identify the 5 largest and 5 smallest TypeI methods
top_5_type1 <- tab_df %>% slice_max(order_by = TypeI, n = 5)
paste0(top_5_type1$Scenario, top_5_type1$Method)
bot_5_type1 <- tab_df %>% slice_min(order_by = TypeI, n = 5)
paste0(bot_5_type1$Scenario, bot_5_type1$Method)

# Identify the 5 largest and 5 smallest TypeII methods
top_5_type2 <- tab_df %>% slice_max(order_by = TypeII, n = 5)
paste0(top_5_type2$Scenario, top_5_type2$Method)
bot_5_type2 <- tab_df %>% slice_min(order_by = TypeII, n = 5)
paste0(bot_5_type2$Scenario, bot_5_type2$Method)

# Identify the 5 largest and 5 smallest TPR methods
top_5_TPR <- tab_df %>% slice_max(order_by = TPR, n = 5)
paste0(top_5_TPR$Scenario, top_5_TPR$Method)
bot_5_TPR <- tab_df %>% slice_min(order_by = TPR, n = 5)
paste0(bot_5_TPR$Scenario, bot_5_TPR$Method)

# Identify the 5 largest and 5 smallest FPR methods
top_5_FPR <- tab_df %>% slice_max(order_by = FPR, n = 5)
paste0(top_5_FPR$Scenario, top_5_FPR$Method)
bot_5_FPR <- tab_df %>% slice_min(order_by = FPR, n = 5)
paste0(bot_5_FPR$Scenario, bot_5_FPR$Method)

```
